name: Customer pays the final invoice after job completion
description: Job is complete. Final invoice (balance after deposit) goes out. Customer pays. The job record now shows fully paid; outstanding KPI for the contractor returns to baseline.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.context: Diego finished the fence repair this morning. Final invoice for $322.50 (balance) follows.

steps.expect: Email shows breakdown: total $430, deposit -$107.50, balance due $322.50

steps.action: Pays via card or Apple Pay
steps.expect: Same payment flow as deposit; success state; receipt emailed

steps.action: Backend marks job paid in full
steps.expect: Job state on /jobs flips to paid; client status moves from 'active' to 'won/recent' on /clients; outstanding KPI on /dashboard decreases by $322.50
Activity feed entry 'Patel paid in full — $322.50'; client card moves out of 'owes' segment

evaluationCriteria.check: Balance breakdown on the public invoice is unambiguous — no customer should call asking 'why is this less than the quote?'
End-of-job feels tidy — receipt + thank-you email closes the loop
Partial payments are supported (e.g. customer pays $200, $122.50 still owed) without breaking the totals
Currency line items use proper aria-label so screen readers announce 'balance due, three hundred twenty-two dollars and fifty cents'
